rulings.Case Title: JESUS THOMAS SOTO VS. PETER LOUIE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enter Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, September 30, 2016 line 1. PLAINTIFF JESUS SOTO's Motion To Enter Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Settlement Agreement GRANTED. Defendants breached the Settlement Agreement. Plaintiffs are entitled to judgment per the terms of the Settlement Agreement. Plaintiffs shall recover attorney's fees in the amount of $3,667.50. = (501/NJL)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Noah J. Lebowitz

rulings.Case Title: BURKE HANSEN VS. JOSH HACHADOURIAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, September 30, 2016 line 2. DEFENDANT HERBERT JAFFE, TCC UNION SQUARE L.P., WILLIAM DIXON, JR.'s DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT continued to October 21, 2016. Parties are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B. Moving party to provide courtesy copy of the operative complaint to department 501 no later than October 7, 2016 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Opposing party to provide courtesy copy of opposition to department 501 no later than October 7, 2016 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. = (501/NJL)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Noah J. Lebowitz

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN MOLTHEN INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA AL-MASRI VS. TOPWELL INVESTMENT INC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, September 30, 2016 line 3. DEFENDANT TOPWELL INVESTMENT INC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and show that it is entitled to a judgment as a matter of law. The Notice of Motion/Motion indicates that this motion is based on a complete affirmative defense as Plaintiff's claims are untimely and barred by CCP 337.15 [sic]. No other grounds listed. See CRC 3.1110, 3.1112. In the Points and Authorities Defendant raises additional grounds: (1) CCP 337 and 338; (2) lack of prohibition against banking the CPI Adjustments; and (3) lack of misrepresentation and lack of allegation of knowledge of falsehood at the time the misrepresentation was made. The latter two grounds are not appropriate for a Motion for Summary Judgment as they fail to dispose of this action. As to the statue of limitations, the moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that a cause of action for a distinct breach of contract accrues on the date of a prior separate and district breach of that contract i.e. that February 1, 1995 alleged violation of the lease (FAC, paragraph 15(A)) triggered the statute of limitations for the separate, distinct, and different breach alleged to have occurred on September 17, 2014 (FAC, paragraph 23). = (501/NJL)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Noah J. Lebowitz

rulings.Case Title: GUI YAN ZHOU ET AL VS. MATTHEW WAI MA, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF THE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On The Applicability Of The San Francisco Rent Ordinance
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, September 30, 2016 line 5. PLAINTIFF GUI ZHOU, DONG HUANG, WEI WU, YU XIE, BEN WU's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On The Applicability Of The San Francisco Rent Ordinance DENIED. The issue proposed for adjudication does not completely dispose of a cause of action, affirmative defense, a claim for damages or an issue of duty. CCP 437c(f). = (501/NJL)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Noah J. Lebowitz
